name: CPersona
description: Persistent character persona memory with FTS5 + vector hybrid search, anti-contamination, and a 24-tool surface. Single SQLite file, zero LLM dependency at the storage layer.

optional_env_vars.name: CPERSONA_DB_PATH
optional_env_vars.description: SQLite database path. Defaults to a relative path; set to an absolute path in production.

optional_env_vars.name: EMBEDDING_MODE
optional_env_vars.description: Embedding mode (none | http | api). Generic key shared with other Cloto MCP servers. Falls back to CPERSONA_EMBEDDING_MODE for backward compatibility.

optional_env_vars.name: EMBEDDING_HTTP_URL
optional_env_vars.description: HTTP-mode embedding endpoint. Required when EMBEDDING_MODE=http. Falls back to CPERSONA_EMBEDDING_URL.

optional_env_vars.name: CPERSONA_RECALL_MODE
optional_env_vars.description: Recall strategy: 'rrf' (Reciprocal Rank Fusion across FTS / vector / keyword) or 'cascade' (sequential fallback).

optional_env_vars.name: CPERSONA_CONFIDENCE_ENABLED
optional_env_vars.description: Include confidence metadata in recall results.

optional_env_vars.name: CPERSONA_AUTO_CALIBRATE
optional_env_vars.description: Auto-calibrate vector similarity threshold on startup using the null-distribution z-score method.

optional_env_vars.name: CPERSONA_RECALL_PRECISION
optional_env_vars.description: Recall precision point (knob 3): 'strict' (fewer contaminants, more misses), 'balanced' (Youden's J), or 'lenient' (fewer misses, more contaminants). Shifts where the calibrated post-fusion gate sits on its data-derived curve. The curve is auto-calibrated; this is the one policy choice.

changelog:
v2.4.27: Extend the recall-precision calibration to the confidence gate (Goal #132 follow-up). v2.4.26 calibrated the post-fusion gate on the rsf/rrf fused score, but _apply_quality_gate evaluates the confidence branch first when CONFIDENCE_ENABLED, so on a confidence-enabled deployment the calibrated gate was bypassed and precision still ran on the pool-size heuristic _adaptive_min_score. The gate calibration now keys on whichever score the gate actually compares — confidence > rsf > cosine > rrf precedence, via _gate_score — and tags the calibrated value with its 'signal'; _apply_quality_gate applies it only to the matching branch, so a gate calibrated under one config (e.g. confidence-on) is simply inert under another with no scale mismatch. The simulate-query calibration now runs the same post-recall scoring as do_recall (episode-boundary penalty + confidence), factored into _apply_recall_scoring, so the operating point is computed on the exact runtime gate score. The startup guard now calibrates the gate when restoring a sidecar that lacks a gate signal (e.g. a v2.4.25 -> v2.4.27 upgrade with unchanged embedding dimension), so the gate activates without waiting for a dimension change. The sidecar stores the gate 'signal' instead of the recall 'mode' (backward compatible — an older sidecar's missing signal triggers a one-time gate calibration at startup). No new tools or env. No schema change (SCHEMA_VERSION stays 10); tool count unchanged at 24.
v2.4.26: Post-fusion quality-gate calibration (recall precision, Goal #132). The v2.4.24/25 vector-threshold calibration was inert in the rsf/rrf fusion modes: the pre-fusion vector floor is scaled by RRF_THRESHOLD_FACTOR below the null mean, and the post-fusion quality gate used a pool-size heuristic (_adaptive_min_score) that never consulted the calibrated distribution, so fusion-mode precision was uncalibrated (the calibration only bit in cascade). This calibrates the post-fusion gate with the same separation method applied to the fused-score distribution. Because fused scores (_rsf_score / _rrf_score) are query-dependent and cannot be derived from stored embeddings alone, the distributions are produced by simulate-query: at calibration time the server samples stored memories as pseudo-queries, runs the live RECALL_MODE fusion pipeline, and separates the fused scores of temporally-adjacent (same-session) rows from unrelated rows at the Youden-J operating point. The gate is per-agent, keyed by embedding dimension, persisted in the calibration sidecar alongside the vector threshold, and applied only when its calibration mode matches the live RECALL_MODE (same score scale). Adds knob 3, the precision point: CPERSONA_RECALL_PRECISION = strict | balanced (default) | lenient maps to a specificity weight (beta) in the separation objective (sensitivity + beta*specificity), shifting where on the calibrated curve the gate sits — the one policy choice the system cannot derive from data. New env: CPERSONA_RECALL_PRECISION / CPERSONA_FUSED_GATE_ENABLED / CPERSONA_FUSED_GATE_SAMPLE_QUERIES / CPERSONA_FUSED_GATE_BETA. Falls back to the pool-size heuristic when disabled, in cascade mode, or when too few samples exist; a pre-2.4.26 calibration sidecar restores the vector threshold only (backward compatible). No schema change (SCHEMA_VERSION stays 10); tool count unchanged at 24.
v2.4.25: Autocut small-result hardening. Autocut now keeps every row when the result set is below CPERSONA_AUTOCUT_MIN_RESULTS (default 3). Under RSF, _minmax_norm pins the lowest-scoring row to 0.0, so a 2-item set always carried a full-scale gap that autocut cut to a single row — discarding a still-relevant second hit (the over-cut that blocked making rsf the default). RRF stays the default; rsf remains opt-in. No schema change (SCHEMA_VERSION stays 10); tool count unchanged at 24.
v2.4.24: Recall-contamination root fix in vector-threshold calibration. The auto-calibration formula had an inverted sign (threshold = mean - z*std), placing the vector-similarity floor BELOW the mean of the random-pair (null) cosine distribution, so most unrelated memories cleared the gate — severe topic drift on anisotropic embedding models (bge-m3 random-pair mean ~0.51). Fixed to mean + z*std and added two methods: 'percentile' (a quantile of the null distribution, distribution-free) and 'separation' (new default) — a two-population operating point maximizing Youden's J between the null and a label-free positive proxy (same-session memories via temporal adjacency, nearest-neighbour fallback), learning the threshold from the corpus's own separability instead of a fixed value. Tier-4 guard: the calibrated threshold is persisted to a sidecar keyed by embedding dimension and restored on restart; an embedding-dimension change (e.g. a silent jina 768d to bge-m3 1024d swap) forces recalibration at startup even when AUTO_CALIBRATE is off — a stale jina-era threshold surviving such a swap was the contamination trigger. New env: CPERSONA_CALIBRATE_METHOD / CPERSONA_CALIBRATE_PERCENTILE / CPERSONA_CALIBRATE_TEMPORAL_WINDOW_MIN / CPERSONA_CALIBRATE_ON_MODEL_CHANGE. calibrate_threshold gains method/percentile args and reports null_admit_rate / youden_j / proxy_source. No schema change (SCHEMA_VERSION stays 10); tool count unchanged at 24.
v2.4.23: Recall-contamination fixes + Relative Score Fusion (opt-in). (Bug A / bug-001) config.py now reads the generic embedding env keys EMBEDDING_MODE / EMBEDDING_HTTP_URL / EMBEDDING_API_* / EMBEDDING_MODEL with the CPERSONA_* key as an override (specific > generic, matching the marketplace catalog and the CScheduler convention) — a catalog-installed instance, which sets the generic keys, previously ran with embeddings silently off and recall degraded to FTS-only. (Bug B / bug-002) the FTS keyword query is now built by decomposing CJK runs into overlapping trigram OR-terms instead of query.split(), which collapsed space-less scripts (Japanese / Chinese) into one phrase the trigram index never matched — the keyword retriever was dead for Japanese, removing the channel RRF needs to separate topically-distinct memories. New CPERSONA_RECALL_MODE=rsf (Relative Score Fusion): per-query min-max-normalizes each channel raw score (cosine / bm25) and sums, so the keyword channel bm25 magnitude survives the merge that RRF rank-only fusion flattens; rrf stays the default, rsf is opt-in and recommended for topic-drift-prone / Japanese contexts. End-to-end bench (full-precision bge-m3): raspberry contamination 9 -> 2 with rsf and no recall loss. No schema change (SCHEMA_VERSION stays 10); tool count unchanged at 24.
v2.4.22: Per-channel episodic loop. Episodes gain a channel column (SCHEMA_VERSION 9 -> 10, in-place ALTER backfill) so archive_episode / recall can scope episodes to one conversation channel (e.g. a Discord channel id); empty channel = unscoped / shared.
v2.4.21: Vendor mcp-common into _vendored_mcp_common to remove the transitive git+url dependency, so a marketplace git-clone install resolves without a separate mcp-common fetch. No schema or tool-surface change.
v2.4.20: Add a source_id prefix filter to recall (json_extract(source, '$.id') LIKE prefix), letting multi-user sessions (e.g. Discord) scope recall to one user / source. No schema change.
v2.4.19: Session no-persist mode. Adds three tools (pause_persistence / resume_persistence / persistence_status) that turn this server's write tools into no-ops for an opt-in TTL window (default 30 min, max 1 day). While paused, store / archive_episode / update_* / delete_* / lock_* / unlock_memory / update_profile / import_memories / merge_memories / calibrate_threshold return responses with `persisted: false`, `dry_run: true`, and `id: "no-persist"` instead of writing. The MemoryTaskQueue worker drops queued archive_episode / update_profile tasks under no-persist. check_health(fix=true) and deep_check(fix=true) downgrade to fix=False during no-persist and report `repairs_skipped: true`. Read tools (recall, list_*, get_profile, etc.) are unaffected. Module-level state per process; server restart loses the flag. Symmetric with cscheduler 0.2.6 — clients should call pause_persistence on both servers if they want both paused. Tool count: 21 → 24.
v2.4.18: Internal refactor — γ-semantics SQL builder and project_id coercer moved to common/isolation.py (shared with cscheduler 0.2.4). No behavior, schema, or tool-surface change.
v2.4.17: agent_id × project_id two-tier isolation (γ semantics). SCHEMA_VERSION 8→9 adds project_id (TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '') to memories / episodes / profiles / pending_memory_tasks via ALTER TABLE backfill — existing DBs are upgraded in place. Writes accept project_id (defaults to '' = global pool when omitted). Reads apply γ filter (None = no filter, '' = global pool only, 'X' = 'X' ∪ global pool). store dedup (msg_id / content) is now project-scoped.
